title: Strata — an optimizing compiler to WebAssembly
description: A from-scratch optimizing compiler that turns a small C-like language into real WebAssembly bytecode in your browser: SSA, SCCP/GVN/LICM/DCE/inlining, if-conversion to branchless select, and a relooper backend. Does floating point from scratch, both directions: str(float) is a genuine Dragon4 shortest-round-trip formatter written in the language itself, and parse_float its correctly-rounded inverse. Now ships a full transcendental math library (exp/ln/log2/log10/sin/cos/tan/asin/acos/atan/atan2/sinh/cosh/tanh/pow/cbrt/hypot/fmod, plus sqrt/floor/round/…) written ONCE as a shared Strata kernel that the wasm backend compiles and the reference interpreter runs — so they agree bit-for-bit, and a dedicated oracle proves each is within ~1 ULP of libm. Plus a real f32 single-precision type lowered end to end to the wasm f32 opcodes (arithmetic, compare, conversions, f32[], f32 struct fields), structs with nested/recursive layouts and a null handle, 64-bit long → wasm i64 (BigInt-exact, hex/L literals, long[], str(long)), bit primitives (popcount/clz/ctz/rotl/rotr), do-while/switch, a string standard library with str[] (split/join), a step debugger, and a differential test suite proving the wasm matches a reference interpreter at every opt level. NEW: first-class functions — a fn(…)->R type whose values are real function pointers, called through a wasm function table and call_indirect, usable in variables/params/returns/struct-field vtables, with a devirtualization pass that turns provable indirect calls back into direct ones at -O1+; this unlocks genuine higher-order programming (map/reduce, a comparator sort, compose). NEWEST: arrays of function pointers — (fn(…)->…)[] via fn_array(n) — a real jump table / state machine, with function-table slot 0 reserved as a null funcref so an unset slot traps on call and fn == null is an observable 'is this wired up?' test; the flagship is a stack-based bytecode VM whose entire decode step is one table[op](vm, imm) lowering to call_indirect. All proven by the differential harness (716 checks across -O0..-O3). NEWEST: division/remainder by a constant — strength reduction that turns every `/ C` and `% C` (32- AND 64-bit) into the textbook multiply/shift/add sequences: power-of-two shifts with a round-toward-zero bias, and the signed magic-number multiply of Hacker's Delight, including a from-scratch 64-bit high-multiply synthesized purely from i64 ops since wasm has no mulhi. At -O1+ the hardware i32.div_s/i64.div_s opcodes vanish entirely, each rewrite an EXACT identity (never an approximation) proven bit-for-bit against the reference interpreter at every level and fuzzed over millions of dividends including INT_MIN/INT_MAX. NEWEST: MEMORY OPTIMIZATION — the first pass to reason about linear memory. An alias analysis (address → base SSA value + constant byte offset; same-base disjoint ranges proven non-aliasing, any different base conservatively may-alias) drives a forward available-memory dataflow (a MUST analysis, meet = intersection, iterated to a fixpoint) that does store→load forwarding (a field written then read back becomes the value), redundant-load elimination (the same element loaded twice loads once), dead-store elimination (a field overwritten before any read isn't stored) and silent-store elimination (writing a value a cell already holds is a no-op). Conservative by precondition — a call clears all facts, print is a read-only barrier, i8 byte stores are never forwarded — so it can only ever miss, never miscompile; a particle step()'s read-modify-write burst collapses from 8 memory ops to 1 at -O1, proven behaviour-preserving by the differential oracle (756 checks across -O0..-O3).
